title: Senior Staff Client Platform Engineer
description_html: NVIDIA has been transforming computer graphics, PC gaming, and accelerated computing for more than 25 years. It’s a unique legacy of innovation that’s fueled by great technology—and amazing people. Today, we’re tapping into the unlimited potential of AI to define the next era of computing. An era in which our GPU acts as the brains of computers, robots, and self-driving cars that can understand the world. Doing what’s never been done before takes vision, innovation, and the world’s best talent. As an NVIDIAN, you’ll be immersed in a diverse, supportive environment where everyone is inspired to do their best work. Come join the team and see how you can make a lasting impact on the world.    This Senior Staff Client Platform Engineer role is a high-impact technical leadership position dedicated to architecting the NVIDIA’s next-generation client platforms. You will move beyond traditional management to build scalable, automated, agentic ecosystems that define the future of global endpoint infrastructure. In this role, you will set the vision for platform service delivery, lead multi-functional initiatives, and drive measurable improvements in reliability, security, and developer productivity through advanced software engineering practices.     What you’ll be doing:     Architect and build scalable tooling and services for client platforms, establishing the end-to-end technical strategy for platform automation and service delivery.    Apply deep codified principles to ensure repeatable, auditable, and version-controlled changes across a global, heterogeneous client environment.    Set and enforce technical standards for platform automation, mentoring other engineers and driving the adoption of modern software engineering practices across IT.    Collaborate with Security, Networking, and Product teams to systematically improve platform reliability and the internal developer experience.    Serve as the primary architect for sophisticated service delivery challenges, driving root-cause analysis and long-term remediation for core platform services.    Influence and recommend changes to policies, standards, and architectural direction, and establish procedures that impact multiple teams or the broader organization.       What we need to see:     BS/MS or equivalent experience in Engineering or Computer Science    12+ years of experience in Client Platform Engineering    Expert-level client platform engineering experience with a proven track record of managing global endpoint fleets at scale.    Mastery of Configuration as Code and Infrastructure as Code methodologies (e.g., Terraform, Ansible, Chef, or Puppet) and Git-based CI/CD workflows.    Demonstrated success in building internal tooling, CLI tools, or microservices that automate complex platform operations.    Strong proficiency in software development (e.g., Python, Go, or Rust) applied specifically to systems engineering and platform tooling.    Strong communication and partner management skills, with the ability to align diverse teams around a common platform vision.       Ways to stand out from the crowd:     Strong experience in Windows client environment engineering, particularly in large-scale enterprise settings.    Familiarity with building AI models or agents to enhance platform automation and self-healing capabilities.    Sincere dedication to the end-user experience, with a history of using data and feedback to drive user-focused platform improvements.    Experience with macOS client platform engineering.      NVIDIA is widely considered to be one of the technology world’s most desirable employers. We have some of the most forward-thinking and hardworking people in the world working for us. If you're creative and autonomous, we want to hear from you!     Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 200,000 USD - 322,000 USD.           You will also be eligible for equity and  benefits .           Applications for this job will be accepted at least until July 24, 2026.   This posting is for an existing vacancy.   NVIDIA uses AI tools in its recruiting processes.   NVIDIA is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment and proud to be an equal opportunity employer. As we highly value diversity in our current and future employees, we do not discriminate (including in our hiring and promotion practices) on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Senior Solutions Architect, Agentic AI
description_html: Do you want to drive the future of AI by building agentic AI applications at scale? We are looking for Solution Architects to join the NVIDIA AI Enterprise (NVAIE) SA Segment Team to help redefine how enterprises build and deploy AI agents. We specialize in the newest technology and advances in Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Generative AI. The vision of the NVAIE Segment team is to use our deep expertise to guide and enable the successful adoption at scale of NVIDIA AI Enterprise Software in production!              What you’ll be doing:     The Agentic AI team mission is to deliver innovative and optimized AI agents using the latest techniques including Test Time Compute, Reinforcement Learning, inference optimization and model fine-tuning. We specialize on engineering new solutions to fit our customers needs by integrating their enterprise data sources into meaningful agentic applications.    You’ll work with agentic frameworks to develop applications that retrieve and generate insights from enterprise data, including text, code, and images. Your focus will be on creating high-impact solutions such as deep research assistants, multi-modal dialogue systems, and task-specific agents that support a wide range of enterprise workflows. You’ll be deeply engaged with engineering teams, stay ahead of the latest AI advancements, and apply strong technical judgment to everything you deliver.    Provide direct feedback from these first-time implementations to improve our software products and scale knowledge by educating vertical teams and building communities on NVIDIA AI software products!       What we need to see:     Strong foundational expertise, from a BS, MS, or Ph.D. degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, Data Science, or similar (or equivalent experience).    12+ years experience demonstrating an established track record in Deep Learning and Machine Learning. Strong software engineering and debugging skills, including experience with Python, C/C++, and Linux. Experience with GPUs as well as expertise in using deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch.    Proficiency in rapid prototyping using Python with strong foundational knowledge of data structures, algorithms, and software engineering principles.    Experience with building advanced multi-agent systems, using libraries like LangGraph, LlamaIndex, CrewAI.    Ability to multitask effectively in a dynamic environment, as well as clear written and oral communications skills with the ability to effectively collaborate with executives and engineering teams.       Ways to stand out from the crowd:     Expertise in building evaluation harnesses, success metrics, automated testing pipelines, and guardrail frameworks to ensure agentic AI workflows are safe, reliable, and production-ready.    Skilled in fine-tuning and optimizing reasoning-focused LLMs and SLMs, including prompt engineering, quantization, and benchmarking.    Experience developing production-grade deployment patterns using Kubernetes/OpenShift, CI/CD automation, and secure cloud-native infrastructure.                  Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 224,000 USD - 356,500 USD.           You will also be eligible for equity and  benefits .           Applications for this job will be accepted at least until July 24, 2026.   This posting is for an existing vacancy.   NVIDIA uses AI tools in its recruiting processes.   NVIDIA is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment and proud to be an equal opportunity employer. As we highly value diversity in our current and future employees, we do not discriminate (including in our hiring and promotion practices) on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Solutions Architect — Power and Utilities, AI Grid, Power Systems
description_html: Join us at NVIDIA, where we are pushing the boundaries of what's possible in the power and utilities sector through innovative AI and accelerated computing solutions. As a Utilities and Energy Sector / Grid AI Solutions Architect, you will work with customers, partners, and fellow NVIDIA engineers to build AI applications and platforms that support grid modernization, simulation, interconnection acceleration, forecasting, resilience, and flexible load orchestration. You will bring to bear NVIDIA technology and Python across the energy and utility industries, impacting transmission and distribution system operators, generation owners, and grid-software vendors.    Are you passionate, dedicated, and creative? Our team seeks a member to advance opportunities in grid simulation, forecasting, optimization, and applied AI. You will become a trusted technical advisor, connecting our industry teams, partners, and customers. Engaging with internal developers, researchers, data scientists, and senior leaders is a core part of the role, offering exposure to a wide range of partners and challenges!     What you will be doing:     Partner with our industry and account teams to understand customer goals, strategies, and technical needs, and to define and deliver high-value, GPU-accelerated grid solutions that meet them.    Build and prototype solutions for specific grid problems such as interconnection-study and queue acceleration, load and renewable-generation forecasting, DER/DERMS orchestration, power-flow and contingency analysis at scale, predictive asset maintenance, wildfire and grid-resilience analytics, and data-center demand flexibility (e.g., EPRI DCFlex).    Help develop industry direction by integrating NVIDIA technology into AI, HPC, and enterprise GPU and networking architectures for grid applications. These range from control-center and planning workloads in the datacenter to real-time analytics at the grid edge and substation.    Strategically partner with flagship customers and industry-specific solution partners targeting our computing platform.    Collaborate primarily through virtual tools, with up to 20% travel required.       What we need to see:     MS or PhD in Electrical or Power Systems Engineering, Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Physics, or related experience (or equivalent experience).    Extensive practical understanding of the power and utilities sector and power-system basics, with direct experience in grid applications and tasks including SCADA/EMS/ADMS/DERMS, power-flow and state estimation, contingency and stability analysis, load and renewable forecasting, and grid asset management.    Hands-on experience with power-system modeling and simulation tools such as pandapower, OpenDSS, GridLAB-D, MATPOWER/PYPOWER, PSS®E, PSCAD, PowerWorld, DIgSILENT PowerFactory, or MATLAB/Simulink, including real-time and hardware-in-the-loop platforms such as RTDS, OPAL-RT, or Typhoon HIL.    Ability to translate power-system problems into GPU-accelerated workflows using NVIDIA's accelerated-computing and AI stack (CUDA, RAPIDS/cuGraph, cuOpt, Modulus, Omniverse, Earth-2, and Triton/TensorRT).    Familiarity with grid data models, standards, and protocols — CIM (IEC 61970/61968), IEC 61850, DNP3, and OPC UA — along with utility data systems like OSIsoft/AVEVA PI historians and Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.    Familiarity with grid operations and planning, interconnection, ISO/RTO markets, and the NERC/FERC regulatory environment.    Over 4 years of professional experience in power-systems engineering, data science, or software development — or similar background — including solid Python skills and familiarity with GPU parallel computing.    Experience with modern Deep Learning software architecture and frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow) and the Python data-science ecosystem (NumPy, pandas, RAPIDS).    Comfortable with modern application-deployment practices such as Docker/Containers and Kubernetes.       Ways to stand out from the crowd:     CUDA and GPGPU programming and optimization experience.    Applied data science and machine-learning R&D on power-systems or grid data — load/price forecasting, renewable integration, outage prediction, anomaly detection, or graph neural networks on grid topology.    Creative generation of synthetic data — synthetic load profiles, grid topologies, or fault scenarios — to augment unusual grid datasets and stress-test models.    Experience with network software development.      With competitive salaries and a generous benefits package, NVIDIA is recognized as one of the technology world’s most sought-after employers. This role offers a chance to make a broad impact at NVIDIA by advancing innovation within our power and utilities sector. Are you inventive, diligent, committed, and driven? Do you enjoy tackling challenges? If so, we want to hear from you!     Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 152,000 USD - 241,500 USD for Level 3, and 184,000 USD - 287,500 USD for Level 4.           You will also be eligible for equity and  benefits .           Applications for this job will be accepted at least until July 24, 2026.   This posting is for an existing vacancy.   NVIDIA uses AI tools in its recruiting processes.   NVIDIA is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment and proud to be an equal opportunity employer. As we highly value diversity in our current and future employees, we do not discriminate (including in our hiring and promotion practices) on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or any other characteristic protected by law.
